title: HTML5 APIs (canvas, video, audio)
summary: HTML5 added built in features browsers can use without extra plugins.
The canvas element lets you draw graphics with code.
The video and audio elements play media directly.
These features work in the browser on their own.

sections.text: Older websites needed clunky add ons to play video or draw graphics. HTML5 built these powers right into the browser, so they just work. Here are a few of the most useful.

sections.title: What HTML5 APIs Are
sections.text: An API is a set of ready made features you can use. HTML5 added several that live inside the browser itself. No downloads and no plugins. You write a little code and the browser handles the rest.

sections.title: Canvas, Video, and Audio
sections.text: The canvas element is a blank drawing surface where code can paint shapes, images, even simple games. The video and audio elements play media directly in the page with built in controls. All three are part of the browser now.

media.alt: A canvas drawing, a video player, and an audio player together

sections.title: Tools Built Into the Toolbox
sections.text: It used to be that to play a video you had to buy a separate gadget and plug it in. HTML5 is like a toolbox that already has those tools built in. Open the lid and they are right there, ready to use.

media.alt: A toolbox opening to reveal built in media and drawing tools

sections.title: Using an HTML5 Feature
items: Pick the element you need, like canvas, video, or audio.
Add it to your page where you want it.
For media, point it at the file and turn on controls.
For canvas, use a little JavaScript to draw on it.
The browser runs it with no extra plugins.

sections.text: Many browser based games and drawing apps run entirely on the canvas element. What looks like a complex app is often code painting on one simple surface.

terms.definition: The modern version of HTML with built in features.
A set of ready made features you can use in code.
A blank surface where code can draw graphics.
The element that plays a movie in the page.
The element that plays sound in the page.

questions.text: What is one thing HTML5 added?
options: Built in features like canvas, video, and audio without plugins
questions.explanation: HTML5 added built in features like canvas, video, and audio that work without extra plugins.

options: A blank surface where code can draw graphics
questions.explanation: The canvas element is a blank drawing surface that code can paint on.

questions.text: Do HTML5 video and audio need extra plugins?
options: No, they work in the browser on their own
questions.explanation: HTML5 video and audio play directly in the browser with no extra plugins.
